text:
Day six of theOlympic Gamesis in full swing with 18 gold medal events taking place across an action-packed schedule inParis.

Mathilda Hodgkins-Byrne and Becky Wilde secured bronze forTeam GBin the opening rowing final of the day but there was heartbreak for Helen Glover, who missed out on what would have been a historic gold in the women’s four.

Great Britain missed out on first placeby the smallest of margins with Netherlands finishing ahead of them in first.

Andy Murray’s superb surge in Pariswill continue alongside Dan Evans with the pair in doubles quarter-final action against Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul.

Golf action is finally underway with Tommy Fleetwood and Matt Fitzpatrick representing Great Britain andRory McIlroy and Shane Lowry flying the Irish flag.

And after an exemplary first few days in Paris – marked by leading Team USA to gold on Tuesday – Simone Biles is back in action in the women’s individual all-around gymnastics final.

Elsewhere, another American star in Katie Ledecky has the chance to make more history in the pool.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

Andy Murray’s doubles defeat – and subsequent retirement from professional tennis – is the big headline of the day, but there’s plenty more coming up…

Join us again in the morning for all this:

Here’severything Andy Murray saidafter his retirement from professional men’s tennis was confirmed on Thursday with his doubles defeat in Paris.

Never change, Andy Murray…

Never even liked tennis anyway.

What a wild ride it’s been â¤@andy_murrayAnd we’ve loved every minute of it ð¥¹pic.twitter.com/vD67SAYDsG

Andy Murray, speaking to the BBC: ‘I gave as much as I could to the sport and I love competing in the Olympic Games. These weeks are so special and I will definitely miss them – I have done everything I could to prepare for this event.

‘It would have been a fairytale ending to win a medal, but it wasn’t to be. Proud to be a part of Team GB and I will miss it.’

What does retirement look like for Andy Murray?There won’t be many lie-ins, that’s for sure! ð¤£#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/MKXQqXMZlv

Andy Murray has always had a brilliant sense of humour.

He’s been cracking jokes in his last media interview as a player – and says he is going straight back to the Olympic Village formore pin collecting!

“You’re a legend.” ð«¡You’re not wrong, kid â¤#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/1wa9PIOhoF

Andy Murray was left in tears at the end of the match. Very emotional scenes in the French capital.

“I think you better say something.” ð­Clare Balding paid an emotional tribute to Andy Murray after he played the final match of his career â¤#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/IkWwJoylpW

TheBBCstudio at theParisOlympicsfell into silence and an emotionalClare Baldingnearly stopped broadcasting overAndy Murray’s official retirement.

Click here for the full story.

An emotional farewell from Andy Murray ð¥¹Thank you, Andy â¤#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/MO01RB7iyh

The Lawn Tennis Association (LTA) has issued a statement over Andy Murray’s official retirement.

‘The LTA is committed to working with Andy and the Murray family to ensure that his career is suitably recognised,’ they said in a statement.

‘As part of this the LTA have committed £5m to support Judy Murray’s plans to build a tennis centre of national significance for Scotland at the Park of Keir, near Dunblane. The LTA is also working with Government and other partners in ensuring Andy’s achievements and his legacy will benefit future generations for years to come.

‘More immediately the LTA is today announcing that from 2025 the centre court seating arena at the LTA’s London Championships held at The Queen’s Club will be known as‘The Andy Murray Arena’.

‘Andy has a special relationship with the tournament, having won the event more than any other in his career, and more times than any other player in the Championships’ 123-year history. The new name for the arena will be used at both the WTA 500 and ATP 500 events to be held at the venue from 2025.’

‘The legacy Andy Murray leaves will continue to impact British tennis for decades to come,’ British tennis player Naomi Broady told the BBC.

Andy Murrayhas officially retired from tennis after playing his final professional match at the 2024Olympic Gamesin Paris next toDan Evansin doubles.

Full story here.

Andy Murray and Dan Evans suffer a men’s doubles quarter-final defeat to Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul – 6-2 6-4.

It means Murray’s tennis career is over.

Wow. Wow. Wow.

Murray and Evans hold. They are not finished here by any means…

The Brits are 5-4 behind – and will level up the set if they can conjure up a break here.

Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul will win the match if they hold their nerve, though. It’s on a knife-edge. If the Americans win, Murray’s career is over.

My word…

Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul won the following game to lead 5-2 and look on the verge of victory – but Andy Murray is doing Andy Murray things again – as he and Dan Evans saved a match point and secured the break to make it 5-3.

The Brits are back on serve, with a chance to make it 5-4 and put some real pressure on the Americans. This isn’t over yet!

Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul took a 4-1 lead but Team GB have just secured their first break of the match.

Andy Murray and Dan Evans trail 4-2 – but they are now back on serve. They couldn’t… could they?

Andy Murray’s career looks like it is coming to a brutal end here… unless he and Dan Evans can somehow turn this quarter-final around.

Murray and Evans lost the first set, and now Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul have moved ahead in the second with a break.

Team USA currently lead 6-2 3-1.

Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul win the first set 6-2.

Andy Murray and Dan Evans, once again, need to produce something special here to keep their hopes of a medal alive and slam the brakes on the veteran’s retirement.

If Team GB lose, it is all over for Murray.

Four games into this first set. Andy Murray and Dan Evans have lost all of them. Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul lead 4-0 at things stand…

The Brits are really up against it now… Team USA have conjured up a double break and lead 3-0 after winning the first three games in a row.

Are we in for another mind-bending comeback here – or is this finally going to be the end of Andy Murray’s career?

SIMONE BILES HAS WON HER SIXTH GOLD MEDAL ðShe’s won the women’s all-around gymnastics title ð¥#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/zwFdysKK4K

It’s gold for Simone Biles and the crowd go absolutely wild!

We would say it was never in doubt but in reality it was…

The American superstar was pushed all the way, particularly by Rebecca Andrade and Sunisa Lee, but delivered when it mattered most.

Brazil’s Andrade takes silver and America’s Lee gets bronze. That’s Biles second gold of the Paris Olympics (she could get another FOUR).

Not the best start for the Team GB duo… Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul get the break in game one and then hold to take a 2-0 lead in the first set.

It was a superb winner from American star Fritz, too.

ArrowMORE:Olympic tennis ball girl goes viral for giving Novak Djokovic a flirty look

description: Today's Paris 2024 Olympics schedule and results for Rowing, Golf, Tennis, Gymnastics, Swimming, Badminton, Hockey and Canoe Slalom.
keywords: Sport,Andy Murray,Dan Evans,Helen Glover,Olympics,Rory McIlroy,Rowing,Simone Biles,Team GB
